channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 26th June
item.description:
Shards of sharp slate pieces (potentially from fallen roofing tiles) on the carrieageway - double tyre blow out has occurred at around 10:20.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: See photo, 26th June
item.description:
Opposite side of the road to the maida vale turning, by ar cook / forbes george / CS glass.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mead Road, Leckhampton, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over hanging tree and breaking pavement, 26th June
item.description:
Out side Number 30 Benhall Avenue, Cheltenham GL51 6AE Tree over hang drive and branches falling on to drive, car and ME! Pavement is now so lifted from tree root that my father tripped. The roots are beginning to lift our concrete slab driveway.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Benhall Avenue, Hatherley, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The vegetation is too high to see into the roundabout, 26th June
item.description:
No one has cut the vegetation on the roundabout for months and it's so high that all vehicles coming down the slip road at gradual speed are having to fully stop to be able to see into the roundabout if it's safe to proceed. Please can this been sorted ASAP
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Golden Valley Junction, Badgeworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Gl33rf, 25th June
item.description:
Missing double yellows opposite junction which is allowing dangerous parking. Which has caused many near misses. Kingscroft Road directly opposite Porchester Road Junction GL33RF

item.title: Raised tree roots causing trip hazard, 25th June
item.description:
There is a tree and lots of roots protruding in the pavement right outside a black gate on the left before the bus stop.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 133, Saint Georges Road, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham, Cheltenham

item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: The Public Footpath (EBW2) is illegally blockked, 25th June
item.description:
The footpath (PROW) is being blocked. There is no access to the path walking north from St. George's churchyard. I have not checked the northern boundary of Brockworth Court, but I suspect that a similar illegal closure has been put in place at that point.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Court Road, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 25th June
item.description:
Overgrown tree causing obstruction.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees implicated in subsidence of adjacent building, 25th June
item.description:
Subsidence Damage at Nuffield Health Gloucester Fitness & Wellbeing Gym, Centre Seven, Barnwood, GL4 3HR (Land Registry tile No. GR327038 - Land at A40, Barnwood, Gloucester) We act on behalf of the property owners in relation to a claim for suspected subsidence damage affecting the above property, in conjunction with their insurers. Our initial investigations identified a group of trees, believed to be maintained by National Highways on land owned by Gloucester City Council, as the principal cause of, or a material contributor to, the suspected subsidence cracking at the property. This was confirmed in the attached report by MWA Arboriculture Ltd (ref. SUB250918-16673). In addition to the arborist’s report, we have enclosed a report from Curtins Consulting Ltd dated 23rd of October 2025 (ref. 080427-CUR), together with a further report and quotation from Catalyst dated 5 November 2025 (ref. 135520). Structural monitoring has now commenced and all trees and vegetation within the property boundary identified by the arborist for removal were addressed. In the first instance, please arrange for the TG2 group to be removed to create a clear minimum distance of 15 metres from the structure in-line with the arborist’s recommendations, please also confirm that the TG2 group will be maintained to prevent future overgrowth. We understand that the land in question forms a part of the adopted highways areas and, as such, comes under the jurisdiction of the Gloucestershire Highways Authority and any claims of this nature should be directed to you. Please also note that this letter can be produced as evidence in future that you have been notified of the damage being caused by this vegetation and advised to take appropriate action and abate the nuisance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A417, Elmbridge, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 25th June
item.description:
Overgrown Hedge Impeding FW Approx 20m in Length Standard TM - Footway Closure - Alternative Footway Available (Overgrown Hedge)

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 25th June
item.description:
